title: Neural Roto Assistant
description: A Python script integrated into Nuke that automates rotoscoping of complex hair using PyTorch models. It reduces manual labor by 60%.

title: Custom VEX Fluid Solver
description: Our new fluid solver is essentially the Marie Kondo of Houdini—it thanks your cache for its service, then lovingly deletes 40% of it without anyone asking questions. Finally, a tool that respects your hard drive’s boundaries more than your last relationship. Visual fidelity remains so intact that your supervisor will still blame you for the fire being “too fire-y,” but at least they can’t blame the cache size. It’s like a miracle weight-loss plan for your /flipbook folder: all the pyro goodness, none of the existential dread when you check disk space. Now you can tell the farm “the solver is running an aggressive compression pass” while you’re actually in the parking lot eating a croissant, and nobody will question it. We polled ten senior FX artists about what they’d do with 40% more free space—nine said “store even more wedges,” and one whispered “finally install Baldur’s Gate 3.” The solver achieves its magic through a proprietary blend of black-market math and the deep-seated knowledge that nobody needs 800GB of foam to sell shampoo. Other solvers promise “lossless efficiency”; ours just gives you back the hours you used to spend explaining to IT why the NAS is wheezing like an asthmatic pug. It’s so unreasonably compact that your laptop can now run a production-scale fluid sim, which is fortunate because the render farm is busy rendering someone else’s 37th lens-flare pass. Best of all, it reduces cache size by 40% with zero visual sacrifice—so you can keep obsessing over surface tension details while quietly pretending you’ve finally learned to let go.

title: Auto-Lighting Pipeline
description: Machine learning model that analyzes 3D scenes and suggests optimal HDRI lighting setups based on reference photos.
